posted.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
posted.tweet: Anthropic built a technique giving the clearest view yet of what's happening *inside* Claude as it thinks — and what they found ranges from mundane to unnerving.… https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/07/09/1140293/anthropic-found-a-hidden-space-where-claude-puzzles-over-concepts

posted.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
posted.tweet: Anthropic just launched Cowork — a Claude Desktop agent that operates directly in your files, no coding needed. Built in ~10 days, largely by Claude itself. Agentic AI… https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-launches-cowork-a-claude-desktop-agent-that-works-in-your-files-no

posted.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
posted.tweet: CISA wrote its incident response playbook while responding to the incident. Every team laughs at that until they check whether their own runbooks exist before the pager goes off.

posted.title: We scaled PgBouncer to 4x throughput
posted.tweet: SO REUSEPORT multi-process PgBouncer breaks on query cancellation. Cancel requests arrive on a new connection and the kernel may route them to the wrong process.

posted.title: Show HN: Mindwalk – Replay coding-agent sessions on a 3D map of your codebase
posted.tweet: Coding agents touch more of your codebase than you think. Mindwalk replays the session as a 3D map where edited files glow and untouched areas stay dark. Scope creep becomes visible before you merge.

posted.title: AI Boosts Research Careers but Flattens Scientific Discovery
posted.tweet: AI tools make individual researchers more productive. A study of 40 million papers found they also narrow what science gets done. More output, less range. That tradeoff probably runs through your codebase too.

posted.title: Claude Code sends 33k tokens before reading the prompt; OpenCode sends 7k
posted.tweet: Claude Code sends 33k tokens before your prompt. OpenCode sends 7k. OpenCode caches once. Claude Code rewrites its cache mid-session, generating up to 54x more cache write tokens. Cache writes cost extra. That adds up.

posted.title: Show HN: Adaptive Recall, persistent memory for AI assistants over MCP
posted.tweet: Running four retrieval strategies in parallel plus a knowledge graph plus ACT-R cognitive scoring sounds thorough. It also sounds like four ways to add latency with no published numbers showing it beats a well-tuned vector search baseline.

posted.title: Billion Dollar PDFs
posted.tweet: 117 documents that moved billions of dollars. Only 16 were technical papers. 24 were VC doctrine. 9 were pitch decks. Engineers write the papers. Someone else writes the narrative that allocates the money.

posted.title: Designing and assembling my first PCB
posted.tweet: First PCB project pattern: wire it on a breadboard, pick one protocol (I2C, not SPI), copy the datasheet schematic into KiCad, and build only what you actually use. Scope reduction is the skill, not the compromise.

posted.title: Beavis Ultrasound PnP ISA Sound Card Replica
posted.tweet: Someone fully reverse-engineered the Gravis Ultrasound PnP down to the GAL logic, published the schematic and KiCad files, and the whole thing hinges on one AMD chip that may not exist anymore. Complete documentation, zero buildable units.

posted.title: Show HN: DOM-docx – HTML to native, editable Word docs (MIT)
posted.tweet: dom-docx validates HTML-to-Word output by rendering in Chromium, converting, rasterizing via LibreOffice, then scoring against human-approved layouts. Most converters skip that loop and just hope for the best.

posted.title: Show HN: PlanWright – A control plane for AI coding agents
posted.tweet: Coding agents made the coding fast. Now the bottleneck is the human on either end. Planning and acceptance review run at human pace and that's where agentic pipelines actually stall.

posted.title: Show HN: Sx 2.0 – Share AI skills with your team through a Dropbox folder
posted.tweet: SX 2.0 shares AI skills via a cloud folder. No server, no accounts. Works great until you need access control or an audit trail. Then the folder is the server, and it has none of that.

posted.title: Show HN: I RL-trained an agent that trains models with RL (for –$1.3k)
posted.tweet: Someone RL-trained an agent to write RL training jobs, reward went from 0.0 to 0.63 over 54 steps, it transferred to held-out tasks, total cost was $1,300. The meta loop is closed. The price of that experiment is now a rounding error.

posted.title: Launch HN: Agnost AI (YC S26) – Extract user feedback from agent conversations
posted.tweet: Your evals pass. Users are still cursing at the agent, rephrasing the same question four times, then leaving anyway. That gap is where the actual failures live, and most teams never look at it.

posted.title: Show HN: Grepathy – Claude made a decision nobody approved
posted.tweet: Claude pre-created guest users in Clerk on a contract project. No plan, no spec, just the agent deciding. The developer couldn't explain it to the CTO because he hadn't made the call. And the reasoning was gone in 30 days anyway.

posted.title: Show HN: Painterly – Turn pictures into digital paintings without generative AI
posted.tweet: 51,750 brush strokes, each accepted only if it improves the image. No diffusion model, no latent space. Slower, but fully deterministic and auditable. Sometimes the boring algorithm is the right one.

posted.title: LLM Networking with MikroTik
posted.tweet: SSH text piping breaks in subtle ways when an LLM is driving. REST/JSON API is the cleaner call. Structured commands, predictable surface, less silent failure.

posted.title: Open Source, Free Tier Capable Whispr Using Cloudflare AI
posted.tweet: Durable Objects exist specifically because plain Workers can't hold WebSocket state. Using one to stream PCM audio chunks to Whisper is a small architectural decision that makes the whole free-tier backend actually work.

posted.title: Where are YC founders now? OpenAI and Anthropic, mostly
posted.tweet: 105 YC founders ended up at OpenAI or Anthropic after their startups. 60% hold the same title: Member of Technical Staff. The labs are now the soft landing for the YC ecosystem.

posted.title: UIUC AI Teaching Assistant
posted.tweet: 11 models in parallel, 2-second median response. Multi-model pipelines are not an architecture problem. They are a coordination and wait-time problem.

posted.title: Codex Resets
posted.tweet: 35 resets in 35 weeks. Cache misses during session compaction, reliability incidents, usage consumed faster than anyone modeled. The mechanism was different each time. That is the lesson.

posted.title: Transcribe.cpp
posted.tweet: whisper.cpp works until you need a model it doesn't support. ONNX covers more models but drops GPU acceleration. transcribe.cpp is one ggml engine, 60+ validated models, Vulkan/Metal/CUDA. The gap was real and someone finally filled it.

posted.title: Scrying the AMD GFX1250 LLVM Tea Leaves
posted.tweet: AMD's MI455X runs Wave32 only. Every CDNA kernel tuned for Wave64 occupancy targets, register blocking, accumulator layout, starts over. The hardware ships soon and most porting guides don't reflect this yet.

posted.title: AI advice made people 3x less accurate but 2x confident, researchers found
posted.tweet: AI availability dropped willingness to say 'I don't know' from 44% to 3%. Accuracy fell by 3x. Confidence doubled. You didn't build an assistant. You built a confidence pump with a broken floor.

posted.title: Delete Your Data with Drop
posted.tweet: California now has a single-request deletion platform for data brokers. One consumer request, your pipeline has to respond. If you build anything that sells or shares consumer data, the compliance surface just got a name and a portal.

posted.title: LoRA Speedrun – a public wall-clock leaderboard for fine-tuning techniques
posted.tweet: Sequence packing and completion-only loss masking cut LoRA fine-tune time from 11m57s to 6m05s with zero changes to the LoRA config. Accuracy went up, not down. The bottleneck was the data pipeline the whole time.

posted.title: Agent swarms and the new model economics
posted.tweet: Cursor's swarm insight: the win is context hygiene, not parallelism. Planners never implement, workers never plan. Mixed cheap workers with a frontier planner hit 80% of a SQL suite in four hours.

posted.title: Judge approves $1.5B Anthropic settlement for pirated books used to train Claude
posted.tweet: AP News reports a $1.5B settlement over pirated books used to train Claude. Not yet corroborated. Worth watching if you work on training data provenance.

posted.title: Gemini last models: temperature, top_p, and top_k are deprecated and ignored
posted.tweet: Gemini 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite silently ignore temperature, top-p, and top-k. Your code won't break visibly. The output just stops behaving as tuned. Audit before you assume.

posted.title: Six questions before you add an LLM
posted.tweet: Gave one LLM agent the full pipeline. Web search, deduplication, validation, database insertion. 10-20% error rate. 800+ records needing manual review. The mistake wasn't using an LLM. It was not asking whether that step even needed one.

posted.title: Are AI Labs Pelicanmaxxing?
posted.tweet: To test benchmark overfitting, build a grid: 8 animals by 6 vehicles, 3 samples each at temp 1.0. Score animal quality, vehicle quality, and action coherence separately. One suspiciously strong cell tells you what leaderboards hide.
